executive_summary: The Iran–US armed conflict has escalated to MAJOR severity following a US-Israeli preventive strike on Iran (2026-02-28) that killed Supreme Leader Ayatollah Khamenei and senior IRGC officials. Iran has responded with coordinated ballistic missile and drone strikes on 14+ US bases across Iraq, Syria, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and the Gulf, plus attacks on Israel. The conflict has expanded to include direct Iranian strikes on UAE (Dubai) and Bahrain, with civilian casualties reported across all theaters. Regional powers are summoning ambassadors, Iraq has closed airspace, and international condemnation is mounting.
whats_new: IRGC announces seventh and eighth waves of 'Al-Wa'd Al-Sadeq 4' operations against US bases — indicating sustained retaliatory campaign (source: Al Alam, 2026-02-28T15:57+03:00)
Iran launches third wave of suicide drones targeting Dubai and Bahrain, hitting a hotel in Bahrain and causing damage to an airport (source: Daily Mail)
US strikes Revolutionary Court building in Tehran — direct escalation against Iranian judicial infrastructure linked to IRGC (source: Hezbollah Electronic Army TG)
Iranian missiles strike Greek Cyprus hosting British military personnel — potential expansion to Eastern Mediterranean theater (source: Daily Sabah)
Kuwait condemns Iranian attacks on Oman; GCC condemns Iranian aggression targeting Duqm Port and oil tanker near Oman (source: QNA General)
Saudi Arabia summons Iran's ambassador in response to attacks on Saudi territory and other regional countries (source: QNA General)
confirmed_events: Khamenei killed in US-Israeli strike on Tehran compound — confirmed by SCMP, Ynet, Daily Sabah, Jerusalem Post, Middle East Eye
US and Israel launched coordinated airstrikes on 20+ Iranian provinces targeting government facilities and IRGC centers (2026-02-28T06:16 UTC)
Iran launched ballistic missile barrage on Israel (Haifa, Galilee) — confirmed by multiple sources
Iran struck 14 US military bases across Iraq, Syria, and Gulf region with coordinated missile/drone attacks (2026-02-28T17:30+03:00)
US Navy support ship struck by IRGC missiles in Gulf region — confirmed naval engagement
Iranian strikes on US base in Erbil, Iraq — confirmed
School strike in southern Iran (Minab) killed at least 80-85 children — confirmed by multiple sources
Iranian missile/drone attacks on Dubai and Bahrain targeting Western tourists — confirmed by multiple sources
Iraq extended airspace closure for additional 24 hours — confirmed operational impact
unverified_emerging: IRGC claims 200 US military personnel killed in Iranian strikes on American bases — unverified claim, no US confirmation (source: IRGC statement)
Former Iranian President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad reportedly killed in Tehran strike — reported by Ynet, RIA Novosti, RBC, War Monitors but unconfirmed; some reports conflict on location
Death toll figures from Red Crescent (747 injured, 201 killed) — unverified, source unspecified
Iranian claim of using civilian areas as cover for military targets — unverified, contested by US sources
Pakistan PM trip to Russia cancelled due to regional clashes — reported by The Hindu but unverified
Serbia deploying military specialists to UAE for strike analysis — reported by TASS, unverified

people_travel: Iraq has extended airspace closure for additional 24 hours, impacting regional air travel
131 Hong Kong residents stranded in Middle East due to flight cancellations (SCMP)
Dubai tourism disrupted — tourists at luxury resorts forced to seek shelter in underground car parks during missile launches
Israel closed Rafah crossing and all West Bank/Gaza checkpoints
supply_chain: Iraqi airspace closure disrupting regional logistics and supply routes
Gulf shipping routes under threat — Duqm Port in Oman attacked, oil tanker targeted off Omani coast
UK troops and civilians at risk at base in Bahrain from Iranian strikes — direct impact on coalition maritime operations
Critical infrastructure targeted: US radar in Qatar destroyed, Bahraini hotel hit, Dubai hotel and airport damaged
market_macro: Oil markets under pressure from Gulf region attacks on ports and tankers
International condemnation from China, Pakistan, Russia, and regional states complicating diplomatic resolution
Protest-related unrest in Pakistan and Iraq following Khamenei's death — potential for additional instability affecting regional trade routes
Serbia seeking to analyze UAE strikes for air defense improvements — indicates European interest in conflict implications

outlook_24_72h.base_case: The conflict is likely to continue escalating over the next 24-72 hours. Iran has announced ongoing 'Al-Wa'd Al-Sadeq 4' operations with multiple waves, indicating sustained retaliatory capacity. With Khamenei killed, Iran's leadership structure is in flux but IRGC has demonstrated continued command and control. US bases and assets across the Gulf remain primary targets. Regional states (Saudi Arabia, UAE, Kuwait, Bahrain, Oman) are directly engaged via strikes on their territory, increasing likelihood of their deeper involvement. Israel faces continued ballistic missile threats. The conflict trajectory points toward further expansion rather than de-escalation.
escalation_risks: Iranian attacks on Greek Cyprus could draw UK/NATO into direct conflict
Potential for Iranian strikes on additional Gulf state infrastructure — ports, oil facilities, airports
Risk of IRGC using civilian human shields as cover — contested claim but escalation risk if confirmed
Hezbollah rally in Beirut could trigger additional front opening
Pakistani-Iranian border tensions (clashes at Bagram) could expand conflict southward
UK forces in Bahrain already struck — risk of UK military response
Russian/Chinese diplomatic involvement could complicate resolution but also provide off-ramps
outlook_24_72h.de_escalation_pathways: De-escalation requires either: (1) significant US decision to limit strikes and reopen diplomatic channels — unlikely given current momentum; (2) Iranian decision to cease retaliation after demonstrating capacity — IRGC statements suggest intent to continue; (3) regional pressure from Gulf states on both parties — but Gulf states are themselves targets; (4) international mediation (China, Russia) offering face-saving off-ramps — China has condemned the attack and called for ceasefire, suggesting potential intermediary role. The death of Khamenei creates power vacuum that may delay any negotiated settlement as Iran consolidates new leadership.
